fault_hypothesis: On line 73, the expiration check uses strict greater-than (`now > expires_at`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= expires_at`), so entries queried at exactly their expiration time are incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: At the expiry check, when now equals expires_at the condition should be true (entry is expired); the strict > misses the equality case.
Detect when now equals expires_at so the boundary condition can be checked; the correct branch should treat equality as expired.
After the expiry branch is NOT taken (entry returned as valid), now must be strictly less than expires_at; equality should have triggered expiry.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or (key in entries and now < expires_at__1)

constraints.intent: The function should only return a non-None value when now is strictly less than expires_at.
